text.content:
In the world of material handling, forklift safety is non-negotiable.

With OSHA reporting that a significant percentage of forklift accidents involve instability and tip over incidents, the role of proper tires and safety procedures is paramount. Each collision or serious injury represents a profound human and financial cost. This guide outlines essential safety tips, focusing on how tire selection and proper training create a foundation for workplace safety and prevent costly collisions.

text.content: Five Pillars of Forklift Tire Safety

rich_text.plain_text: Five Pillars of Forklift Tire Safety

text.content: relies on a system built on five key factors:

rich_text.plain_text: relies on a system built on five key factors:

text.content: Floor conditions, loading docks, inclines, and debris directly impact traction and tire wear. Maintaining clear visibility and marking walkways are critical.

rich_text.plain_text: Floor conditions, loading docks, inclines, and debris directly impact traction and tire wear. Maintaining clear visibility and marking walkways are critical.

text.content: Matching tire type to the application and conducting pre-shift inspections of tires, overhead guard, and safety features prevents failures.

rich_text.plain_text: Matching tire type to the application and conducting pre-shift inspections of tires, overhead guard, and safety features prevents failures.

text.content: OSHA-mandated forklift training is the first defense. This includes understanding load capacity, avoiding overloading, and respecting speed limits.

rich_text.plain_text: OSHA-mandated forklift training is the first defense. This includes understanding load capacity, avoiding overloading, and respecting speed limits.

text.content: Enforcing safety rules like using the seat belt, setting the parking brake, and sounding the horn at intersections protects everyone.

rich_text.plain_text: Enforcing safety rules like using the seat belt, setting the parking brake, and sounding the horn at intersections protects everyone.

text.content: Organized layouts minimize sharp turns and obstructions, reducing tire stress and potential collisions.

rich_text.plain_text: Organized layouts minimize sharp turns and obstructions, reducing tire stress and potential collisions.

text.content: Exiting the stability triangle, defined by the rear wheels and the center of the front axle.

text.content: Traveling on inclines with or without heavy loads, which shifts the center of gravity.

rich_text.plain_text: Traveling on inclines with or without heavy loads, which shifts the center of gravity.

text.content: Making sharp turns at speed, especially with a raised load.

rich_text.plain_text: Making sharp turns at speed, especially with a raised load.

text.content: Always operate within the machine’s rated load capacity.

rich_text.plain_text: Always operate within the machine’s rated load capacity.

text.content: Keep loads low and tilted back during the direction of travel.

rich_text.plain_text: Keep loads low and tilted back during the direction of travel.

text.content: Drive slowly on ramps, always pointing the load upgrade.

rich_text.plain_text: Drive slowly on ramps, always pointing the load upgrade.

text.content: Maintain a safe distance from edges and loading docks.

rich_text.plain_text: Maintain a safe distance from edges and loading docks.

text.content: Pre-Operation Inspection: Check tire pressure/wear, test the parking brake, lights, and horn.
